# Pinned, reproducible runtime dependency closure for arbling-telegram-mcp.
#
# Generated by a FRESH resolve inside the digest-pinned python:3.12-slim base
# image (linux/amd64 — the exact environment that consumes this file), so the
# pins always start from the newest releases pyproject.toml's ranges allow.
# Regenerate after a pyproject.toml dependency change:
#
#   docker run --rm --platform linux/amd64 -v "<repo>:/w:ro" \
#     python:3.12-slim@<digest from the Dockerfile FROM line> \
#     sh -c "pip install -q /w; pip freeze --exclude arbling-telegram-mcp"
#
#   ...then re-add the two `sys_platform == "win32"` marker lines below
#   (Windows-only packages never appear in a Linux freeze) and re-run
#   `pip-audit --no-deps -r requirements-lock.txt` — it must report zero
#   known vulnerabilities before the change lands.
#
# Named *requirements-lock.txt* (not requirements.lock) on purpose: Dependabot's
# pip ecosystem only discovers requirements files whose name contains
# "requirements" and ends in .txt/.in, so this name keeps the pinned closure
# inside Dependabot's weekly update radar.
#
# pyproject.toml stays the source of truth for version *ranges*; this file
# pins the exact resolved closure so `docker build` installs byte-identical
# dependency versions every time instead of re-resolving against whatever is
# newest on PyPI at build time.
annotated-types==0.7.0
anyio==4.14.2
attrs==26.1.0
certifi==2026.6.17
cffi==2.1.0
click==8.4.2
# Windows-only transitive dep of click; absent from the Linux freeze. Pinned
# conditionally so Windows dev installs from this lock stay reproducible too.
colorama==0.4.6; sys_platform == "win32"
cryptography==49.0.0
h11==0.16.0
httpcore==1.0.9
httpx==0.28.1
httpx-sse==0.4.3
idna==3.18
jsonschema==4.26.0
jsonschema-specifications==2025.9.1
mcp==1.28.1
pyaes==1.6.1
pyasn1==0.6.4
pycparser==3.0
pydantic==2.13.4
pydantic-settings==2.14.2
pydantic_core==2.46.4
PyJWT==2.13.0
python-dateutil==2.9.0.post0
python-dotenv==1.2.2
python-multipart==0.0.32
# Windows-only (no Linux distribution exists); pulled in transitively by mcp
# only when sys_platform == "win32" — kept pinned + conditional so it never
# gets installed on the linux/amd64 Docker image.
pywin32==312; sys_platform == "win32"
PyYAML==6.0.3
referencing==0.37.0
rpds-py==2026.6.3
rsa==4.9.1
six==1.17.0
sse-starlette==3.4.6
starlette==1.3.1
Telethon==1.44.0
typing-inspection==0.4.2
typing_extensions==4.16.0
uvicorn==0.51.0
